div.p: The Control chapter defines the fundamental message processing rules, acknowledgment patterns, and infrastructure segments used across all HL7 v2+ domains.
HL7 v2+ defines two primary modes of message exchange:

ul.li: Original Mode — The sending system transmits a message and waits for an acknowledgment (ACK) from the receiving system before sending additional messages.
Enhanced Mode — Provides more granular control over acknowledgment, separating accept acknowledgment (confirming receipt) from application acknowledgment (confirming processing).

div.p: Each trigger event defines its acknowledgment choreography, specifying:

ul.li: Whether an immediate ACK is expected
What message type is returned as the response
Whether original mode and/or enhanced mode acknowledgment is supported

div.p: The MSH (Message Header) segment is present in every HL7 v2+ message and defines critical metadata including:

ul.li: Message type, trigger event, and message structure
Processing ID, version, and character sets

div.p: Trigger events represent real-world occurrences in a healthcare setting that initiate the exchange of messages. Each trigger event defines a specific message type and acknowledgment choreography.
See the complete list of trigger events for all events defined in the v2+ specification.
